title: IDP expands into Malaysia amid shifting student preferences
summary_en: The international education provider, long focused on major Anglophone destinations such as the US, UK, Australia and Canada, is now turning to Malaysia as it emerges as a go-to option for degree-seeking students across Asia.
author: Abhishek Nair
title_cn: 学生偏好转变背景下IDP教育集团拓展马来西亚业务

title: India’s TNE push is exposing a workforce challenge universities are not addressing
summary_en: As India experiences a transnational education (TNE) boom, Annette Ord explores the very real practicalities that institutions often overlook when opening overseas branch campuses.
author: Annette Ord
title_cn: 印度推进跨境教育暴露出高校未予应对的劳动力挑战

title: What’s in Canada’s CIMM report and will it influence IRCC policy?
summary_en: Canada’s Standing Committee on Citizenship and Immigration (CIMM) has unveiled a sweeping set of recommendations on the International Student Program (ISP). But how much will they influence IRCC policy?
author: Polly Nash
title_cn: 加拿大下议院公民及移民常设委员会报告包含哪些内容，是否会影响移民、难民及公民部政策？

title: Malvern St James under threat of closure as financial pressures mount
summary_en: UK K-12 boarding school Malvern St James has announced proposals to close at the end of the academic year, against a backdrop of increasing costs and a tax imposed on independent school fees.
author: Beth Kennedy
title_cn: 财务压力不断加大 莫尔文圣詹姆斯学校面临关停风险

title: Study abroad think tank launches in Japan
summary_en: The JAOS Study Abroad Research Institute (JSARI) aims to serve as an “educational compass” to support Japan’s 2033 mobility goals, as outbound numbers stagnate and data gaps persist.
author: Abhishek Nair

title: Ulster University cuts 450 jobs as international revenue falls
summary_en: Ulster University has announced plans to cut up to 450 jobs, as a significant drop in international student revenue pushes the institution into a deepening financial crisis.
author: Charlotte Greenstein
title_cn: 阿尔斯特大学因国际收入下降裁减450个工作岗位

title: Brazil eyes bigger global role as global student flows shift
summary_en: With demand for international education rising and students increasingly favouring non-traditional destinations, Brazil is striving for a greater piece of the pie – positioning itself as a “bridge” between education systems in the global North and South.
author: Polly Nash
title_cn: 全球学生流动格局发生转变 巴西谋求发挥更大全球作用

title: StudyIn acquires Canadian international education consultancy OzTREKK
summary_en: StudyIn has revealed it is adding Canadian international education consultancy OzTREKK to its portfolio, strengthening the group's ANZ recruitment capabilities as it continues its global expansion.
author: Beth Kennedy
title_cn: StudyIn收购加拿大国际教育咨询机构OzTREKK

title: IHE 2026 takeaways: India’s TNE push amid calls for clarity
summary_en: At the Higher Education Leaders Roundtable at IHE 2026, Symbiosis International University, delegates discussed democratising TNE in India, regulatory expectations, the need for stronger executive action, and the case for a South Asia-focused regional approach to internationalisation.
author: Abhishek Nair
title_cn: 2026年国际高等教育大会核心要点：各方呼吁规则明晰 印度推进跨境教育发展

title: Come fly with me: what happened when 125 CEOs, VCs and the UK PM flew to India
summary_en: Here’s what happened when UK university leaders joined Keir Starmer on a 36‑hour dash to Mumbai and found themselves at the centre of India’s higher education revolution.
author: Kim Martin
title_cn: 随我同行：125名首席执行官、风险投资家及英国首相赴印行程相关情况

title: UK universities warned of rising competition in Asia
summary_en: New data has revealed South Asian students are increasingly pivoting away from the UK in favour of Asian destinations, as stakeholders warn of concurrent demand and supply-side challenges.
author: Polly Nash
title_cn: 英国高校被警示亚洲地区竞争日趋激烈

title: Over-50s drive language travel growth as learning becomes a lifestyle choice
summary_en: Language travel has long been associated with younger learners. But a fast-growing demographic – the over-50s – is redefining who study abroad is for.
author: Kim Martin
title_cn: 学习成为生活方式选择 50岁以上群体推动语言游学增长

title: Indian students urged to check UK voting eligibility ahead of tonight’s deadline
summary_en: The National Indian Students and Alumni Union UK (NISAU) has launched a campaign, My Vote Matters, encouraging Indian students residing in the UK to check their eligibility to vote in the upcoming local elections.
author: Charlotte Greenstein
title_cn: 敦促印度学生于今晚截止日期前核查英国投票资格

title: Revealed: Kaplan Languages Group to be bought by Inspirit Capital
summary_en: Inspirit Capital – a UK-based investment company specialising in corporate carveouts – is acquiring language-learning giant Kaplan Languages Group (KLG) from Kaplan, The PIE can reveal.
author: Beth Kennedy
title_cn: 消息披露：楷博语言集团将被英飞睿资本收购
